---
title: "manuscript_figure"
author: "Xindi Lin"
date: "2025-10-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
library(tidyverse)
library(ranger)
library(SuperLearner)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(WeightIt)
library(GpGp)
library(Metrics)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(ggplot2)
library(see)
library(yardstick)
library(xgboost)
library(glmnet)
library(kernlab)
library(glmnet)
library(numDeriv)
library(MASS)
library(tigris)
library(rnaturalearthdata)
library(e1071)
library(broom)
library(stringr) # For str_remove_all
library(gratia) # gam plot smooth term
library(ggeffects)
```

```{r}
source('data_generation.R')
source('funcs_kriging.R')
source('loss_functions.R')
source('loss_functions_RKHS.R')
source('miscellaneous.R')
```



#### calculate rate of passing threshold at every county

```{r}
data <- load_nitrate_data(zero_inflated = TRUE)
```

#### censor 5 years and average

```{r}
data <- load_nitrate_data(zero_inflated = FALSE)
```

get data in the past five years. If a well is measured multiple times, use average/median of the measurement

```{r}
dim(data)
```

```{r}
sum(data$SampleYear>=2020)
data <- data[data$SampleYear>=2020, ]
```

```{r}
data <- data %>%
  group_by(WellDepth, latitude, longitude) %>%
  mutate(
    concentration_plus_mean_five_years = mean(concentration_plus_median, na.rm = TRUE),
    concentration_plus_median_five_years = median(concentration_plus_median, na.rm = TRUE),
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
data <- data %>%
  group_by(WellDepth, latitude, longitude) %>%
  # Keep the row with the latest date within each group
  slice_max(order_by = SampleYear, n = 1) %>%
  ungroup()
```


##### get latest data and exceedance rate

If we do this, it should not be zero-inflated

To do this, we should keep most recent measurement of each well

We will first separate the historical data and most recent data

```{r}
data_latest <- get_latest_record(data)
```

```{r}
data_latest_historical <- get_latest_and_hitorical_nitrate_record(data)
data_historical <- data_latest_historical$historical_nitrate_record
```

```{r}
exceedance_rate_10 <- data_latest %>% 
  group_by(County) %>% 
  summarise(
    exceedance_rate_10 = mean(concentration_plus_median>=10),
    .groups = 'drop'  # This is good practice to ungroup the result
  )
```

```{r}
exceedance_rate_5 <- data_latest %>% 
  group_by(County) %>% 
  summarise(
    exceedance_rate_5 = mean(concentration_plus_median>=5),
    .groups = 'drop'  # This is good practice to ungroup the result
  )
```

##### visualization

```{r}
# this requires rnaturalearthdata
counties <- counties(state = "WI", cb = TRUE, year = 2022) # 'cb = TRUE' for cartographic boundary (simplified) version
counties = counties[,c("NAME","geometry")]
colnames(counties) = c("County","geometry")
```

```{r}
counties = left_join(counties,exceedance_rate_10,by="County")
counties = left_join(counties,exceedance_rate_5,by="County")
```


```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = counties,aes(fill=exceedance_rate_10))+
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Blues",
                        name = 'Exceedance Rate',
                        direction = 1, 
                        labels = scales::percent)+
  labs(title = "Exceedance Rate 10mg/L", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()

ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = counties,aes(fill=exceedance_rate_5))+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma")+
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Blues",
                        name = 'Exceedance Rate',
                        direction = 1, 
                        labels = scales::percent)+
  labs(title = "Exceedance Rate 5mg/L", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```


Central, South Central, West Central are the most severe


#### generate required well depth for the whole state

```{r}
# rename it for the use of the function that has been defined
data$concentration_plus_median <- data$concentration_plus_median_five_years
```

```{r}
data_split <- split_nitrate_data(data)
```

###### outcome regression

```{r}
outcome_regression <- outcome_regression_SVM(data = data_split$data, data_test = data_split$data_test,tunning = FALSE)
```

```{r}
cat('Training: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression$pred + outcome_regression$krige_values)
Metrics::rmse(data_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression$pred)
cat('\n')
cat('Test: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression$pred_test + outcome_regression$krige_values_test)
Metrics::rmse(data_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression$pred_test)
```

Training: [1] 0.782782
[1] 0.8808793

Test: [1] 0.8007543
[1] 0.9051775

This is way too large. It is better to separate them (This is my initial thoughts). But the reason is not we estimate it as a whole. It is because we only keep 

```{r}
plot(data_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression$pred_test)
abline(0,1,col='red')
```

I think this looks way too bad.

```{r}
depth_range <- c(min(data_split$data$logWellDepth), max(data_split$data$logWellDepth))

smoothers <- get_smoothers_RKHS(design_matrix = outcome_regression$design_matrix,
                                              design_matrix_test = outcome_regression$design_matrix_test,
                                              svm_auto = outcome_regression$svm,
                                              depth_range = depth_range)
```

```{r}
saveRDS(smoothers, "smoothers_all_five_years_median.rds")
```

###### indirect method

```{r}
threshold_val = log(5)

indirect_policy_RKHS_south_west <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_south_west$smoothers_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_south_west$krige_values, spatial = TRUE)

indirect_policy_test_RKHS_south_west <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_south_west$smoothers_test_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_south_west$krige_values_test, spatial = TRUE)
```

```{r}
cat('Indirect Training data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_south_west_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_south_west_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_RKHS_south_west,
              threshold_val = log(5))

cat('Indirect Test data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_south_west_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_south_west_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_test_RKHS_south_west,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

###### propensity

####### covariate balance

```{r}
weights_cbps_south_west <- weightit(logWellDepth ~ StaticLevel+crop_type_combine+drainagecl+precipitation+cafolog, 
                                       data = data_south_west_split$data, method = "cbps")

summary(weights_cbps_south_west$weights)
```

###### initialization

```{r}
initial_value_set <- c(depth_range_south_west[1] - 1, mean(depth_range_south_west), depth_range_south_west[2] + 1)
kernel_bw_south_west <- 1.06 * sd(data_south_west_split$data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data_south_west_split$data)^(-1/5)


direct_individual_multi_initial_south_west <- mcmapply(FUN = find_best_policy, 
                                                          i = 1:nrow(data_south_west_split$data), 
                                                          MoreArgs = list(initial_value_set = initial_value_set, 
                                                                          kernel_bw = kernel_bw_south_west,
                                                                          outcome_regression_object = outcome_regression_south_west, 
                                                                          data = data_south_west_split$data, 
                                                                          weights = weights_cbps_south_west$weights,
                                                                          smoothers = smoothers_south_west, 
                                                                          depth_range = depth_range_south, 
                                                                          threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                                          clip_epsilon = 20), SIMPLIFY = TRUE)
saveRDS(direct_individual_multi_initial_south_west, "direct_individual_multi_initial_south_west.rds")


print_acc_mcc(T = data_south_west_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_south_west_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = direct_individual_multi_initial_south_west,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
saveRDS(direct_individual_multi_initial_south_west, "direct_individual_multi_initial_south_west.rds")
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_south_west_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_south_west_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = direct_individual_multi_initial_south_west,
              threshold_val = log(5))

```
              
              
#### generate well depth at different area

```{r}
data <- load_nitrate_data(zero_inflated = FALSE)
```

```{r}
table(data$area)
```

```{r}
data <- data[data$SampleYear>=2019,]
```

```{r}
table(data$area)
```

```{r}
data_central <- data[data$area=="Central",]
data_south_central <- data[data$area=="South Central",]
data_west_central <- data[data$area=="West Central",]
data_east_central <- data[data$area=="East Central",]
data_south_west <- data[data$area=="South West",]
data_low_risk <- data[data$area %in% c("South West","East Central","West Central","Central","South Central"),]
```

```{r}
dim(data_low_risk)
```


##### generate well depth at center

###### outcome regression

```{r}
data_central_split <- split_nitrate_data(data_central)
```

```{r}
outcome_regression_central <- outcome_regression_SVM(data = data_central_split$data, data_test = data_central_split$data_test,tunning = FALSE)
# outcome_regression_central <- outcome_regression_SVM(data = data_central_split$data, data_test = data_central_split$data_test,tunning = TRUE)
```

I found that when we tune the SVM, the performance at test dataset become worse.
With tunning: the result is Training: [1] 0.5618343, [1] 0.5756266. Test: [1] 0.792658, [1] 0.8238066
Without tunning: the result is Training: [1] 0.7460349 [1] 0.816856. Test: [1] 0.7658335, [1] 0.8454641

```{r}
cat('Training: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_central$pred + outcome_regression_central$krige_values)
Metrics::rmse(data_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_central$pred)
cat('\n')
cat('Test: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_central$pred_test + outcome_regression_central$krige_values_test)
Metrics::rmse(data_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_central$pred_test)
```

###### smoothers

```{r}
depth_range_center <- c(min(data_central_split$data$logWellDepth), max(data_central_split$data$logWellDepth))
```

```{r}
smoothers_central <- get_smoothers_RKHS(design_matrix = outcome_regression_central$design_matrix,
                   design_matrix_test = outcome_regression_central$design_matrix_test,
                   svm_auto = outcome_regression_central$svm,
                   depth_range = depth_range)
```

The above code is very slow, so we save the result

```{r}
saveRDS(smoothers_central, "smoothers_central.rds")
```

###### indirect method

```{r}
threshold_val = log(5)
```

```{r}
indirect_policy_RKHS_central <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_central$smoothers_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_central$krige_values, spatial = TRUE)

indirect_policy_test_RKHS_central <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_central$smoothers_test_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_central$krige_values_test, spatial = TRUE)
```

```{r}
cat('Indirect Training data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_RKHS_central,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
cat('Indirect Test data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_test_RKHS_central,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```


###### propensity

####### covariate balance

It worked after I delete the well use, I think it might because the well use is not a balance covariate?

```{r}
weights_cbps_central <- weightit(logWellDepth ~ StaticLevel+crop_type_combine+drainagecl+precipitation+cafolog, 
                         data = data_central_split$data, method = "cbps")
```


```{r}
summary(weights_cbps_central$weights)
```

####### conditional density

```{r}
generalized_propensity_central <- get_generalized_propensity(data = data_central_split$data)
```
```{r}
pmax(generalized_propensity_central$gps_est, 0.0001)
```


###### initialization

```{r}
depth_range_center <- c(min(data_central_split$data$logWellDepth), max(data_central_split$data$logWellDepth))
initial_value_set_central <- c(depth_range_center[1] - 1, mean(depth_range_center), depth_range_center[2] + 1)
kernel_bw_central <- 1.06 * sd(data_central_split$data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data_central_split$data)^(-1/5)
```

```{r}
find_best_policy(i = 1, 
                 initial_value_set = initial_value_set_central, 
                 kernel_bw = kernel_bw_central,
                 outcome_regression_object = outcome_regression_central, 
                 data = data_central_split$data, 
                 # weights_cbps = weights_cbps_central, 
                 weights = 1/pmax(generalized_propensity_central$gps_est, 0.0001), 
                 smoothers = smoothers_central, 
                 depth_range = depth_range_center, 
                 threshold_val = log(5), 
                 clip_epsilon = 20)
```


```{r}
direct_individual_multi_initial_central <- mcmapply(FUN = find_best_policy, 
                                            i = 1:nrow(data_central_split$data), 
                                            MoreArgs = list(initial_value_set = initial_value_set_central, 
                                                             kernel_bw = kernel_bw_central,
                                                             outcome_regression_object = outcome_regression_central, 
                                                             data = data_central_split$data, 
                                                             # weights = 1/pmax(generalized_propensity_central$gps_est, 0.0001),
                                                             weights = weights_cbps_central$weights,
                                                             smoothers = smoothers_central, 
                                                             depth_range = depth_range_center, 
                                                             threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                             clip_epsilon = 20), SIMPLIFY = TRUE)
```

This also takes a very long time

```{r}
saveRDS(direct_individual_multi_initial_central, "direct_individual_multi_initial_central.rds")
```


```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = direct_individual_multi_initial_central,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

This is usually high, but it is commonly overfitted.

###### select kernel bandwidth

```{r}
# there is no well use, and staticlevel 
RKHS_covariate_names = c("StaticLevel", "crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
rec <- recipe(~ ., data = data_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names]) %>%
    step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
    prep() # This "fits" the preprocessor

```

```{r}
kernel_design_matrix <- bake(rec, new_data = data_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names])
kernel_design_matrix <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix, U = outcome_regression_central$krige_values)

kernel_design_matrix_test <- bake(rec, new_data = data_central_split$data_test[, RKHS_covariate_names])
kernel_design_matrix_test <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix_test, U = outcome_regression_central$krige_values_test)

train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
for(m in c(-4,-3,-2.5)){
  cat("m is: ",m,'\n')
  gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix_central))
  rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
  k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
  k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)
  
  k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
  k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

  initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = direct_individual_multi_initial_central, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
  params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
  params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
  cat('training: ')
  print_acc_mcc(T = data_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
                Y = data_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
                policy = k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
                threshold_val = log(5))
  
  cat('test: ')
  print_acc_mcc(T = data_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
                Y = data_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
                policy = k_matrix_test %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
                threshold_val = log(5))
  
}

```
m is:  -4 
training: The accuracy is  0.8967643 
The mcc is  0.7484882 
test: The accuracy is  0.7783619 
The mcc is  0.4261796 
m is:  -3 
training: The accuracy is  0.8639327 
The mcc is  0.6647144 
test: The accuracy is  0.7825125 
The mcc is  0.4455178 
m is:  -2.5 
training: The accuracy is  0.8435463 
The mcc is  0.6119922 
test: The accuracy is  0.7885999 
The mcc is  0.4633573 
m is:  -2 
training: The accuracy is  0.8299158 
The mcc is  0.5757814 
test: The accuracy is  0.7844494 
The mcc is  0.4543024 
m is:  -1.5 
training: The accuracy is  0.8122555 
The mcc is  0.5289164 
test: The accuracy is  0.7822357 
The mcc is  0.4490317 
m is:  -1 
training: The accuracy is  0.7998104 
The mcc is  0.4965716 
test: The accuracy is  0.781959 
The mcc is  0.4469434 
 
Using U is much better than using coordinate.
The static water level is a very important covariate

We would choose m to be -2.5

###### non-DC algorithm

```{r}
# cat("lambda is: ",lambda,'\n')
m <- -2.5
lambda <- 0.25
clip_epsilon <- 30
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(fields::rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)

k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = direct_individual_multi_initial_central, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)

kernel_optim_trt_DB_central <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet, 
    fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
    gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
    K = k_matrix,
    # K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data_central_split$data)), diag(nrow(data_central_split$data))),
    # K = diag(nrow(data)),
    T = data_central_split$data$logWellDepth,
    krige_adjust = outcome_regression_central$krige_values,
    outcome_resid = data_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median - outcome_regression_central$pred - outcome_regression_central$krige_values,
    # propensity_est = pmax(generalized_propensity_central$gps_est, 0.0001),
    propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps_central$weights,
    lambda = lambda, 
    smoothers = smoothers_central$smoothers_RKHS,
    cumint_smoothers = smoothers_central$cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
    trt_bounds = depth_range_center,
    threshold_val = log(5), 
    kernel_bw = kernel_bw_central, 
    clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
    surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
    loss_type = "db",
    method = "L-BFGS-B",
    control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) 
```


```{r}
cat('Training: \n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = k_matrix %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_central$par,
              threshold_val = log(5))
cat('Test: \n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
            Y = data_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
            policy = k_matrix_test %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_central$par, 
            threshold_val = log(5))
```


###### visualize depth map

we need to get the plss covariate

```{r}
file_path <- "/Users/xindilin/Desktop/2024 summer/groundwater_pesticide/data/plss_covariates.csv"
command <- paste("brctl download", shQuote(file_path))
system(command)
plss_covariates <- read.csv(file_path)
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_central <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Wood", "Portage", "Waupaca", "Juneau", 
                                        "Adams", "Waushara", "Marquette", "Green Lake"),]
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_central$cafolog <- log(plss_covariates_central$cafo + 1)
```

```{r}
dim(plss_covariates_central)
dim(na.omit(plss_covariates_central))
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_central <- na.omit(plss_covariates_central)
```

There are one plss section with 'Other Crop', and one with 'Vegtables' as their crop_type_combine, which is present in training data, we will just remove them

```{r}
plss_covariates_central <- plss_covariates_central[!plss_covariates_central$crop_type_combine %in% c("Other Crop"),]
```


####### Interpolate static water level

```{r}
gpfit_staticLevel_central <- GpGp::fit_model(data_central$StaticLevel,
                               locs = data_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")],
                               covfun_name = "matern_sphere")

plss_covariates_central$StaticLevel <-  GpGp::predictions(fit = gpfit_staticLevel_central,
                  locs_pred = plss_covariates_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")], 
                  X_pred = rep(1,nrow(plss_covariates_central)))
```

####### get kriging values for plss

```{r}
plss_krige_values_central <- GpGp::predictions(fit = outcome_regression_central$gpfit, locs_pred = plss_covariates_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")], X_pred = rep(1,nrow(plss_covariates_central)))
```

####### build kernel matrices

```{r}
RKHS_covariate_names = c("StaticLevel", "crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
rec <- recipe(~ ., data = data_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names]) %>%
    step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
    prep() # This "fits" the preprocessor
```

```{r}
plss_kernel_design_matrix_central <- bake(rec, new_data = plss_covariates_central[,  RKHS_covariate_names])

plss_kernel_design_matrix_central <- cbind(plss_kernel_design_matrix_central, U = plss_krige_values_central)
plss_kernel_design_matrix_central <- scale(plss_kernel_design_matrix_central, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
plss_k_matrix_central <- kernelMatrix(rbf, plss_kernel_design_matrix_central, kernel_design_matrix)
plss_k_matrix_central <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(plss_k_matrix_central)), plss_k_matrix_central)
```

####### generate map

```{r}
plss_covariates_central$policy <- plss_k_matrix_central %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_central$par
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_central_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'), crs=4326)
```

```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_east_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = counties,color = 'black')+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Minimum Depth (Feet)",
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_central_sf$U = plss_krige_values_central
```


```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf, aes(color = U), size = 0.15) +
  geom_sf(data = counties,color = 'black')+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Estimation of U",
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```


##### generate well depth at west center

###### outcome regression

```{r}
data_west_central_split <- split_nitrate_data(data_west_central)
```

```{r}
outcome_regression_west_central <- outcome_regression_SVM(data = data_west_central_split$data, data_test = data_west_central_split$data_test,tunning = FALSE)
```

```{r}
cat('Training: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_west_central$pred + outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values)
Metrics::rmse(data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_west_central$pred)
cat('\n')
cat('Test: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_west_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_west_central$pred_test + outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values_test)
Metrics::rmse(data_west_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_west_central$pred_test)
```

###### smoothers

```{r}
depth_range <- c(min(data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth), max(data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth))
```

```{r}
smoothers_west_central <- get_smoothers_RKHS(design_matrix = outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix,
                   design_matrix_test = outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix_test,
                   svm_auto = outcome_regression_west_central$svm,
                   depth_range = depth_range)
```

The above code is very slow, so we save the result

```{r}
saveRDS(smoothers_west_central, "smoothers_west_central.rds")
```

###### indirect method

```{r}
threshold_val = log(5)
```

```{r}
indirect_policy_RKHS_west_central <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_west_central$smoothers_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values, spatial = TRUE)

indirect_policy_test_RKHS_west_central <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_west_central$smoothers_test_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values_test, spatial = TRUE)
```

```{r}
cat('Indirect Training data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_RKHS_west_central,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
cat('Indirect Test data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_west_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_test_RKHS_west_central,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```


###### propensity

####### covariate balance

It worked after I delete the well use, I think it might because the well use is not a balance covariate?

```{r}
weights_cbps_west_central <- weightit(logWellDepth ~ StaticLevel+crop_type_combine+drainagecl+precipitation+cafolog, 
                         data = data_west_central_split$data, method = "cbps")
```


```{r}
summary(weights_cbps_west_central$weights)
```

####### conditional density

```{r}
generalized_propensity_west_central <- get_generalized_propensity(data = data_west_central_split$data)
```
```{r}
pmax(generalized_propensity_west_central$gps_est, 0.0001)
```


###### initialization

```{r}
initial_value_set <- c(depth_range[1] - 1, mean(depth_range), depth_range[2] + 1)
kernel_bw_west_central <- 1.06 * sd(data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data_west_central_split$data)^(-1/5)
depth_range <- c(min(data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth), max(data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth))
```

```{r}
find_best_policy(i = 1, 
                 initial_value_set = initial_value_set, 
                 kernel_bw = kernel_bw_west_central,
                 outcome_regression_object = outcome_regression_west_central, 
                 data = data_west_central_split$data, 
                 # weights_cbps = weights_cbps_west_central, 
                 weights = 1/pmax(generalized_propensity_west_central$gps_est, 0.0001), 
                 smoothers = smoothers_west_central, 
                 depth_range = depth_range, 
                 threshold_val = log(5), 
                 clip_epsilon = 20)
```


```{r}
direct_individual_multi_initial_west_central <- mcmapply(FUN = find_best_policy, 
                                            i = 1:nrow(data_west_central_split$data), 
                                            MoreArgs = list(initial_value_set = initial_value_set, 
                                                             kernel_bw = kernel_bw_west_central,
                                                             outcome_regression_object = outcome_regression_west_central, 
                                                             data = data_west_central_split$data, 
                                                             # weights = 1/pmax(generalized_propensity_west_central$gps_est, 0.0001),
                                                             weights = weights_cbps_west_central$weights,
                                                             smoothers = smoothers_west_central, 
                                                             depth_range = depth_range, 
                                                             threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                             clip_epsilon = 20), SIMPLIFY = TRUE)
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = direct_individual_multi_initial_west_central,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```


###### select kernel bandwidth


```{r}
# there is no well use, and staticlevel 
RKHS_covariate_names = c("StaticLevel", "crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
rec <- recipe(~ ., data = data_west_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names]) %>%
    step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
    prep() # This "fits" the preprocessor

```

```{r}
# kernel_design_matrix <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix
# kernel_design_matrix$logWellDepth <- NULL
# kernel_design_matrix <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values)
# 
# kernel_design_matrix_test <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix_test
# kernel_design_matrix_test$logWellDepth <- NULL
# kernel_design_matrix_test <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix_test, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values_test)
```

```{r}
kernel_design_matrix <- bake(rec, new_data = data_west_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names])
kernel_design_matrix <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values)

kernel_design_matrix_test <- bake(rec, new_data = data_west_central_split$data_test[, RKHS_covariate_names])
kernel_design_matrix_test <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix_test, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values_test)

train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
for(m in c(-3,-2,-1.5,-1)){
  cat("m is: ",m,'\n')
  gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
  rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
  k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
  k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)
  
  k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
  k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

  initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = direct_individual_multi_initial_west_central, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
  params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
  params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
  cat('training: ')
  print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
                Y = data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
                policy = k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
                threshold_val = log(5))
  
  cat('test: ')
  print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
                Y = data_west_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
                policy = k_matrix_test %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
                threshold_val = log(5))
  
}
```
 
Using U is much better than using coordinate.
The static water level is a very important covariate


###### non-DC algorithm

```{r}
kernel_design_matrix <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix
kernel_design_matrix$logWellDepth <- NULL
kernel_design_matrix <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values)

kernel_design_matrix_test <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix_test
kernel_design_matrix_test$logWellDepth <- NULL
kernel_design_matrix_test <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix_test, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values_test)

train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
# cat("lambda is: ",lambda,'\n')
m <- -3
lambda <- 0.25
clip_epsilon <- 30
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(fields::rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)

k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = direct_individual_multi_initial_west_central, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)

kernel_optim_trt_DB_west_central <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet, 
    fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
    gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
    K = k_matrix,
    # K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data_west_central_split$data)), diag(nrow(data_west_central_split$data))),
    # K = diag(nrow(data)),
    T = data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth,
    krige_adjust = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values,
    outcome_resid = data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median - outcome_regression_west_central$pred - outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values,
    # propensity_est = pmax(generalized_propensity_west_central$gps_est, 0.0001),
    propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps_west_central$weights,
    lambda = lambda, 
    smoothers = smoothers_west_central$smoothers_RKHS,
    cumint_smoothers = smoothers_west_central$cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
    trt_bounds = depth_range,
    threshold_val = log(5), 
    kernel_bw = kernel_bw_west_central, 
    clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
    surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
    loss_type = "db",
    method = "L-BFGS-B",
    control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) 
```


```{r}
cat('Training: \n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = k_matrix %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_west_central$par,
              threshold_val = log(5))
cat('Test: \n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
            Y = data_west_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
            policy = k_matrix_test %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_west_central$par, 
            threshold_val = log(5))
```


###### visualize depth map

we need to get the plss covariate

```{r}
file_path <- "/Users/xindilin/Desktop/2024 summer/groundwater_pesticide/data/plss_covariates.csv"
command <- paste("brctl download", shQuote(file_path))
system(command)
plss_covariates <- read.csv(file_path)
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_west_central <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Pepin", "Jackson", "Buffalo", "Trempealeau", 
                                            "La Crosse", "Monroe", "Eau Claire", "Dunn", "Pierce", "St. Croix"),]
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_west_central$cafolog <- log(plss_covariates_west_central$cafo + 1)
```

```{r}
dim(plss_covariates_west_central)
dim(na.omit(plss_covariates_west_central))
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_west_central <- na.omit(plss_covariates_west_central)
```

There are one plss section with 'Cover Crop', and one with 'Vegtables' as their crop_type_combine, which is present in training data, we will just remove them

```{r}
plss_covariates_west_central <- plss_covariates_west_central[!plss_covariates_west_central$crop_type_combine %in% c("Cover Crop", "Vegtables"),]
```


####### Interpolate static water level

```{r}
gpfit_staticLevel_west_central <- GpGp::fit_model(data_west_central$StaticLevel,
                               locs = data_west_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")],
                               covfun_name = "matern_sphere")

plss_covariates_west_central$StaticLevel <-  GpGp::predictions(fit = gpfit_staticLevel_west_central,
                  locs_pred = plss_covariates_west_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")], 
                  X_pred = rep(1,nrow(plss_covariates_west_central)))
```

####### get kriging values for plss

```{r}
plss_krige_values_west_central <- GpGp::predictions(fit = outcome_regression_west_central$gpfit, locs_pred = plss_covariates_west_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")], X_pred = rep(1,nrow(plss_covariates_west_central)))
```

####### build kernel matrices

```{r}
RKHS_covariate_names = c("StaticLevel", "crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
rec <- recipe(~ ., data = data_west_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names]) %>%
    step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
    prep() # This "fits" the preprocessor
```

```{r}
plss_kernel_design_matrix_west_central <- bake(rec, new_data = plss_covariates_west_central[,  RKHS_covariate_names])

plss_kernel_design_matrix_west_central <- cbind(plss_kernel_design_matrix_west_central, U = plss_krige_values_west_central)
plss_kernel_design_matrix_west_central <- scale(plss_kernel_design_matrix_west_central, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
plss_k_matrix_west_central <- kernelMatrix(rbf, plss_kernel_design_matrix_west_central, kernel_design_matrix)
plss_k_matrix_west_central <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(plss_k_matrix_west_central)), plss_k_matrix_west_central)
```

####### generate map

```{r}
plss_covariates_west_central$policy <- plss_k_matrix_west_central %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_west_central$par
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_west_central_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_west_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'), crs=4326)
```

```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_east_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.1) +
  geom_sf(data = counties,color = 'black')+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Minimum Depth (Feet)",
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_west_central_sf$U = plss_krige_values_west_central
```


```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color = U), size = 0.15) +
  geom_sf(data = counties,color = 'black')+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Estimation of U",
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```


##### generate well depth at east central

####### outcome regression

```{r}
data_east_central_split <- split_nitrate_data(data_east_central)
```

```{r}
outcome_regression_east_central <- outcome_regression_SVM(data = data_east_central_split$data, data_test = data_east_central_split$data_test)
```


```{r}
cat('Training: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_east_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_east_central$pred + outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values)
Metrics::rmse(data_east_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_east_central$pred)
cat('\n')
cat('Test: ')
Metrics::rmse(data_east_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_east_central$pred_test + outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values_test)
Metrics::rmse(data_east_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, outcome_regression_east_central$pred_test)
```

###### smoothers

```{r}
depth_range <- c(min(data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth),max(data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth))
```

```{r}
smoothers_east_central <- get_smoothers_RKHS(design_matrix = outcome_regression_east_central$design_matrix,
                   design_matrix_test = outcome_regression_east_central$design_matrix_test,
                   svm_auto = outcome_regression_east_central$svm,
                   depth_range = depth_range)
```


###### indirect method

```{r}
threshold_val = log(5)
```

```{r}
indirect_policy_RKHS <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_east_central$smoothers_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values, spatial = TRUE)

indirect_policy_test_RKHS <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_east_central$smoothers_test_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values_test, spatial = TRUE)
```

```{r}
cat('Indirect Training data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_east_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_RKHS,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
cat('Indirect Test data:\n')
print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_east_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = indirect_policy_test_RKHS,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

###### propensity

```{r}
weights_cbps_east_central <- weightit(logWellDepth ~ StaticLevel+WellUse+crop_type_combine+drainagecl+precipitation+cafolog, 
                         data = data_east_central_split$data, method = "cbps")
```

conditional density

```{r}
generalized_propensity_west_central <- get_generalized_propensity(data = data_west_central_split$data)
```

```{r}
pmax(generalized_propensity_west_central$gps_est, 0.0001)
```


###### initialization

```{r}
mean(data_east_central_split$data$concentration_plus_median>=5)
mean(data_east_central_split$data$concentration_plus_median>=10)
```


```{r}
initial_value_set <- c(depth_range[1] - 1, mean(depth_range), depth_range[2] + 1)
kernel_bw_east_central <- 1.06 * sd(data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth) *nrow(data_east_central_split$data)^(-1/5)
```

```{r}
find_best_policy(i = 1, 
                 initial_value_set = initial_value_set, 
                 kernel_bw = kernel_bw,
                 outcome_regression_object = outcome_regression_east_central, 
                 data = data_east_central_split$data, 
                 weights = weights_cbps_east_central$weights,
                 # weights = 1/pmax(generalized_propensity_west_central$gps_est, 0.0001), 
                 smoothers = smoothers_east_central, 
                 depth_range = depth_range, 
                 threshold_val = log(5), 
                 clip_epsilon = 20)
```

```{r}
direct_individual_multi_initial_east_central <- mcmapply(FUN = find_best_policy, 
                                            i = 1:nrow(data_east_central_split$data), 
                                            MoreArgs = list(initial_value_set = initial_value_set, 
                                                             kernel_bw = kernel_bw_east_central,
                                                             outcome_regression_object = outcome_regression_east_central, 
                                                             data = data_east_central_split$data, 
                                                             weights = weights_cbps_east_central$weights,
                                                             smoothers = smoothers_east_central, 
                                                             depth_range = depth_range, 
                                                             threshold_val = log(5), 
                                                             clip_epsilon = 20), SIMPLIFY = TRUE)
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_east_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median,
              policy = direct_individual_multi_initial_east_central,
              threshold_val = log(5))
```


###### select kernel bandwidth

```{r}
# there is no well use, and staticlevel 
RKHS_covariate_names = c("StaticLevel", "crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog","precipitation")
rec <- recipe(~ ., data = data_east_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names]) %>%
    step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
    prep() # This "fits" the preprocessor

```

```{r}
# kernel_design_matrix <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix
# kernel_design_matrix$logWellDepth <- NULL
# kernel_design_matrix <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values)
# 
# kernel_design_matrix_test <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix_test
# kernel_design_matrix_test$logWellDepth <- NULL
# kernel_design_matrix_test <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix_test, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values_test)

kernel_design_matrix <- bake(rec, new_data = data_east_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names])

kernel_design_matrix <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix, U = outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values)


train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)

kernel_design_matrix_test <- bake(rec, new_data = data_east_central_split$data_test[, RKHS_covariate_names])
kernel_design_matrix_test <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix_test, U = outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values_test)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
for(m in c(-3,-2,-1.5,-1)){
  cat("m is: ",m,'\n')
  gamma <- 2^(m) * median(rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
  rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
  k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
  k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)
  
  k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
  k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

  initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = direct_individual_multi_initial_east_central, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
  params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
  params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)
  cat('training: ')
  print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
                Y = data_east_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
                policy = k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
                threshold_val = log(5))
  
  cat('test: ')
  print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
                Y = data_east_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
                policy = k_matrix_test %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
                threshold_val = log(5))
  
}
```
 
Using U is much better than using coordinate. The static water level is a very important covariate
 
We will use m = -4 for complete dataset.
 
```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_west_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_west_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = direct_individual_multi_initial_west_central, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```
 
 
(we will let m be -1), this is for small, incomplete dataset. For large dataset, the required m would be very small.
It is somehow, m should be small so that the matrix is similar to identity matrix

###### non-DC algorithm

```{r}
kernel_design_matrix <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix
kernel_design_matrix$logWellDepth <- NULL
kernel_design_matrix <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values)

kernel_design_matrix_test <- outcome_regression_west_central$design_matrix_test
kernel_design_matrix_test$logWellDepth <- NULL
kernel_design_matrix_test <- cbind(kernel_design_matrix_test, U = outcome_regression_west_central$krige_values_test)

train_means <- colMeans(kernel_design_matrix)
train_sds <- apply(kernel_design_matrix, 2, sd)

kernel_design_matrix <- scale(kernel_design_matrix)
kernel_design_matrix_test <- scale(kernel_design_matrix_test, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
# cat("lambda is: ",lambda,'\n')
m <- -1.5
lambda <- 0.25
clip_epsilon <- 30
gamma <- 2^(m) * median(fields::rdist(kernel_design_matrix))
rbf <- rbfdot(sigma = 1/(gamma^2))
k_matrix <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix)), k_matrix)

k_matrix_test <- kernelMatrix(rbf, kernel_design_matrix_test, kernel_design_matrix)
k_matrix_test <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(k_matrix_test)), k_matrix_test)

initial_glmnet <- glmnet(x = k_matrix[,-1], y = direct_individual_multi_initial_east_central, alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
params_initial_glmnet <- coef(initial_glmnet)
params_initial_glmnet <- as.numeric(params_initial_glmnet)

print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_east_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = k_matrix %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
              threshold_val = log(5))

print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
              Y = data_east_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
              policy = k_matrix_test %*% params_initial_glmnet, 
              threshold_val = log(5))
```

```{r}
kernel_optim_trt_DB_east_central <- optim(par = params_initial_glmnet, 
    fn = compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
    gr = d_compute_total_loss_smooth_RKHS,
    K = k_matrix,
    # K = cbind(rep(1,nrow(data_west_central_split$data)), diag(nrow(data_west_central_split$data))),
    # K = diag(nrow(data)),
    T = data_east_central_split$data$logWellDepth,
    krige_adjust = outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values,
    outcome_resid = data_east_central_split$data$logconcentration_plus_median - outcome_regression_east_central$pred - outcome_regression_east_central$krige_values,
    propensity_est = 1/weights_cbps_east_central$weights,
    lambda = lambda, 
    smoothers = smoothers_east_central$smoothers_RKHS,
    cumint_smoothers = smoothers_east_central$cumint_smoothers_RKHS,
    trt_bounds = depth_range,
    threshold_val = log(5), 
    kernel_bw = kernel_bw, 
    clip_epsilon = clip_epsilon,
    surrogate_type = "Gaussian",
    loss_type = "db",
    method = "L-BFGS-B",
    control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 3)) 
```

```{r}
print_acc_mcc(T = data_east_central_split$data_test$logWellDepth, 
            Y = data_east_central_split$data_test$logconcentration_plus_median, 
            policy = k_matrix_test %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_east_central$par, 
            threshold_val = log(5))
```

###### visualize depth map


```{r}
plss_covariates_east_central <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Outagamie", "Winnebago", "Door", "Fond du Lac", 
                                            "Brown", "Calumet","Sheboygan","Manitowoc","Kewaunee"),]
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_east_central$cafolog <- log(plss_covariates_east_central$cafo + 1)
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_east_central <- na.omit(plss_covariates_east_central)
```


####### Interpolate static water level

```{r}
gpfit_staticLevel_east_central <- GpGp::fit_model(data_east_central$StaticLevel,
                               locs = data_east_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")],
                               covfun_name = "matern_sphere")

plss_covariates_east_central$StaticLevel <-  GpGp::predictions(fit = gpfit_staticLevel_east_central,
                  locs_pred = plss_covariates_east_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")], 
                  X_pred = rep(1,nrow(plss_covariates_east_central)))
```


####### get kriging values for plss

```{r}
plss_krige_values_east_central <- GpGp::predictions(fit = outcome_regression_east_central$gpfit, locs_pred = plss_covariates_east_central[,c("longitude", "latitude")], X_pred = rep(1,nrow(plss_covariates_east_central)))
```

####### build kernel matrices

```{r}
RKHS_covariate_names = c("StaticLevel", "crop_type_combine","drainagecl","precipitation","cafolog")
rec <- recipe(~ ., data = data_east_central_split$data[, RKHS_covariate_names]) %>%
    step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
    prep() # This "fits" the preprocessor
```

```{r}
plss_kernel_design_matrix_east_central <- bake(rec, new_data = plss_covariates_east_central[,  RKHS_covariate_names])

plss_kernel_design_matrix_east_central <- cbind(plss_kernel_design_matrix_east_central, U = plss_krige_values_east_central)
plss_kernel_design_matrix_east_central <- scale(plss_kernel_design_matrix_east_central, center = train_means, scale = train_sds)
```

```{r}
plss_k_matrix_east_central <- kernelMatrix(rbf, plss_kernel_design_matrix_east_central, kernel_design_matrix)
plss_k_matrix_east_central <- cbind(rep(1,nrow(plss_k_matrix_east_central)), plss_k_matrix_east_central)
```

####### generate map

```{r}
plss_covariates_east_central$policy <- plss_k_matrix_east_central %*% kernel_optim_trt_DB_east_central$par
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_east_central_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_east_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'), crs=4326)
```

```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_east_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = counties,color = 'black')+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Minimum Depth (Feet)",
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_east_central_sf$U = plss_krige_values_east_central
```


```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_east_central_sf, aes(color = U), size = 0.15) +
  geom_sf(data = counties,color = 'black')+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Estimation of U",
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

Boxplot restricted to regions

```{r}
ggplot(plss_covariates_east_central, aes(x = crop_type_combine, y = policy)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Policy v.s. Covariate",
       x = "land use",
       y = "minimum well depth")
```

```{r}
ggplot(plss_covariates_east_central, aes(x = precipitation, y = policy)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Policy v.s. Covariate",
       x = "precipitation",
       y = "minimum well depth")
```

```{r}
ggplot(plss_covariates_east_central, aes(x = cafolog, y = policy)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Minimum Well depth by policy",
       x = "cafo",
       y = "minimum well depth")
```


#### Sub-Region Analysis

```{r}

```


##### Whole State

```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_south_west_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_east_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_south_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = counties,color = 'black') +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Minimum Depth (Feet)",
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```


##### visualize covariate at sub-regions

###### data preparation

we get the plss covariate at different sub-region

```{r}
plss_covariates_sf = st_as_sf(plss_covariates,coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
```

```{r}
file_path <- "/Users/xindilin/Desktop/2024 summer/groundwater_pesticide/data/plss_covariates.csv"
command <- paste("brctl download", shQuote(file_path))
system(command)
plss_covariates <- read.csv(file_path)
```

```{r}
# this requires rnaturalearthdata
counties <- counties(state = "WI", cb = TRUE, year = 2022) # 'cb = TRUE' for cartographic boundary (simplified) version
counties = counties[,c("NAME","geometry")]
colnames(counties) = c("County","geometry")
```

```{r}
data_central <- data[data$area=="Central",]
data_south_central <- data[data$area=="South Central",]
data_west_central <- data[data$area=="West Central",]
data_east_central <- data[data$area=="East Central",]
data_south_west <- data[data$area=="South West",]

data_central_sf <- st_as_sf(data_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
data_south_central_sf <- st_as_sf(data_south_central ,coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
data_west_central_sf <- st_as_sf(data_west_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
data_east_central_sf <- st_as_sf(data_east_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
data_south_west_sf <- st_as_sf(data_south_west, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_south_central <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Columbia", "Dodge", "Dane", "Jefferson", 
                                                                               "Green", "Rock"),]

plss_covariates_central <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Wood", "Portage", "Waupaca", "Juneau", 
                                        "Adams", "Waushara", "Marquette", "Green Lake"),]

plss_covariates_east_central <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Outagamie", "Winnebago", "Door", "Fond du Lac", 
                                            "Brown", "Calumet","Sheboygan","Manitowoc","Kewaunee"),]

plss_covariates_west_central <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Pepin", "Jackson", "Buffalo", "Trempealeau", 
                                            "La Crosse", "Monroe", "Eau Claire", "Dunn", "Pierce", "St. Croix"),]

plss_covariates_south_west <- plss_covariates[plss_covariates$County %in% c("Vernon", "Crawford", "Grant", "Richland", 
                                                                            "Sauk", "Iowa", "Lafayette"),]
```

```{r}
plss_covariates_central_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
plss_covariates_south_central_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_south_central ,coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
plss_covariates_west_central_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_west_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
plss_covariates_east_central_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_east_central, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
plss_covariates_south_west_sf <- st_as_sf(plss_covariates_south_west, coords = c('longitude','latitude'),crs=4326)
```


```{r}
counties_south_central <- counties[counties$County %in% c("Columbia", "Dodge", "Dane", "Jefferson", 
                                                                               "Green", "Rock"),]

counties_central <- counties[counties$County %in% c("Wood", "Portage", "Waupaca", "Juneau", 
                                        "Adams", "Waushara", "Marquette", "Green Lake"),]

counties_east_central <- counties[counties$County %in% c("Outagamie", "Winnebago", "Door", "Fond du Lac", 
                                            "Brown", "Calumet","Sheboygan","Manitowoc","Kewaunee"),]

counties_west_central <- counties[counties$County %in% c("Pepin", "Jackson", "Buffalo", "Trempealeau", 
                                            "La Crosse", "Monroe", "Eau Claire", "Dunn", "Pierce", "St. Croix"),]

counties_south_west <- counties[counties$County %in% c("Vernon", "Crawford", "Grant", "Richland", 
                                                                            "Sauk", "Iowa", "Lafayette"),]
```


###### sactter plot of policy v.s. well depth

```{r}
joined_data <- data_central %>%
  inner_join(plss_covariates_central,
             by = c("Township", "Range", "Range.Direction", "Section"))
```

```{r}
scatter_plot <- joined_data[joined_data$concentration_plus_median>=5,] %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = logWellDepth, y = policy)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "darkblue",size = 1) + # Add points
  # geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") + # Optional: Add a linear trend line
  # Specify X-Axis Breaks and Labels
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = log(c(30, 70, 150, 400)), # <-- NEW: Specify raw break points
    labels = function(x) {
      scales::number(exp(x), accuracy = 1)
    }
  ) +

  # Specify Y-Axis Breaks and Labels
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = log(c(20, 100, 400,1500)), # <-- NEW: Specify raw break points
    labels = function(x) {
      scales::number(exp(x), accuracy = 1)
    }
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(3.3,6), ylim = c(2.4,8.5)) + 
  labs(
    title = "Well Depth Policy v.s. Observed Well Depth in Central Area",
    x = "Observed WellDepth (Feet)",
    y = "Well Depth Policy (Feet)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(
    # panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    # panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.5)
  )

# Display the plot
print(scatter_plot)
```

```{r}


# --- Assuming 'joined_data' from the previous step is available ---
# If you are running this independently, you would load and join your data here.

# 2. Restructure the data to a 'long' format
# We'll select the two columns of interest and use pivot_longer() to gather them.
density_data_long <- joined_data[joined_data$concentration_plus_median>=5,] %>%
  dplyr::select(logWellDepth, policy) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(), # Selects all columns (logWellDepth and policy)
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Value"
  )


# 3. Create the density plot
combined_density_plot <- density_data_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Value, fill = Variable)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) + # alpha controls transparency for overlapping areas
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Depth (Feet)", # Change the X-axis title here
    breaks = log(c(20, 100, 400,1500)) ,
    labels = function(x) {
      # Exponentiate the value and format to a clean number
      scales::number(exp(x), accuracy = 1) 
    }
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Density Plot of  Well Depth Policy v.s. Observed Well Depth in Central Area",
    x = "Depth (Feet)",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "Variable"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("logWellDepth" = "darkblue", "policy" = "darkgreen"),
                    labels = c("logWellDepth" = "Well Depth", "policy" = "Policy"))

# Display the plot
print(combined_density_plot)
```

###### plot of indirect method

First, we get the indirect method

```{r}
indirect_policy_central <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_central$smoothers_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range_center, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_central$krige_values, spatial = TRUE)

indirect_policy_test_central <- compute_indirect_policy(smoothers = smoothers_central$smoothers_test_RKHS, trt_bounds = depth_range_center, threshold_val = threshold_val, krige_adjust = outcome_regression_central$krige_values_test, spatial = TRUE)
```

```{r}
indirect_central <- rbind(cbind(data_central_split$data[,c("Township", "Range", "Range.Direction", "Section","longitude", "latitude")], indirect_policy = indirect_policy_central, U = outcome_regression_central$krige_values),
  cbind(data_central_split$data_test[,c("Township", "Range", "Range.Direction", "Section","longitude", "latitude")],  indirect_policy = indirect_policy_test_central, U = outcome_regression_central$krige_values_test))

indirect_central_sf <- st_as_sf(indirect_central,coords = c("longitude", "latitude"),crs = 4326)
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = indirect_central_sf, aes(color = pmin(indirect_policy,log(2000))), size = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = "black", size = 1)+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D",
                        name = "Indirect Well Depth Policy",
                        breaks = log(c(10, 100, 500, 1000, 2000)),
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "Indirect Well Depth in Central Area") +
  theme_void()
  
```

We want to compare the indirect method and direct method

```{r}
policy_comparison_central <- inner_join(indirect_central, plss_covariates_central, by = c("Township", "Range", "Range.Direction", "Section","longitude","latitude"))
policy_comparison_central_sf <- st_as_sf(policy_comparison_central, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"),crs = 4326)
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = policy_comparison_central_sf, aes(color = pmin(indirect_policy,log(2000))), size = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = "black", size = 1)+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D",
                        name = "Feet",
                        breaks = log(c(10, 100, 500, 1000, 2000)),
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "Indirect Well Depth Policy in Central Area") +
  theme_void()

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = policy_comparison_central_sf, aes(color = pmin(policy,log(2000))), size = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = "black", size = 1)+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D",
                        name = "Feet",
                        breaks = log(c(10, 100, 500, 1000, 2000)),
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "Direct Well Depth Policy in Central Area") +
  theme_void()



ggplot() +
  # geom_sf(data = policy_comparison_central_sf, aes(color = exp(pmin(policy,log(2000))) - exp(pmin(indirect_policy,log(2000)))), size = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = policy_comparison_central_sf, aes(color = (pmin(policy,log(2000))) - (pmin(indirect_policy,log(2000)))), size = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = "black", size = 1)+
  scale_color_gradient2(midpoint = 0,
                        low = "blue",
                        mid = "grey90",
                        high = "red",
                        name = "log(Feet) Difference"
                        # breaks = log(c(10, 100, 500, 1000, 2000)),
                        # labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}
                        ) +
  labs(title = "Direct Policy Minus Indirect Policy in Central Area") +
  theme_void()
  
```


###### Visualize the projection of policy of policy on covariates

```{r}
table(policy_comparison_central$crop_type_combine)
```

```{r}
table(policy_comparison_central$drainagecl)
```

We need to consider the baseline category of crop and the category of 

```{r}
# policy_comparison_central$crop_type_combine <- factor(policy_comparison_central$crop_type_combine, levels = c(
#   "Forest",
#   "Undefined",
#   "Corn",
#   "Soybeans",
#   "Grass",
#   "Vegtables",
#   "Developed"
# ))

policy_comparison_central$drainagecl <- factor(policy_comparison_central$drainagecl, levels = c(
  "Very poorly drained",
  "Poorly drained",
  "Somewhat poorly drained",
  "Moderately well drained",
  "Well drained",
  "Somewhat excessively drained",
  "Excessively drained"
))

policy_comparison_central$crop_type_combine <- relevel(policy_comparison_central$crop_type_combine, ref = "Forest")

policy_comparison_central$precipitation_std <- scale(policy_comparison_central$precipitation)
policy_comparison_central$StaticLevel_std <- scale(policy_comparison_central$StaticLevel)
policy_comparison_central$cafolog_std <- scale(policy_comparison_central$precipitation)
```

```{r}
# policy_lm <- lm(policy ~ crop_type_combine + precipitation + StaticLevel + drainagecl + U + cafolog, data = policy_comparison_central, contrasts = list(drainagecl = "contr.poly"))

policy_lm <- lm(policy ~ crop_type_combine + precipitation + StaticLevel + drainagecl + U + cafolog, data = policy_comparison_central)
```

```{r}
policy_gam <- mgcv::gam(policy ~ crop_type_combine + precipitation + StaticLevel + drainagecl + s(U) + cafolog, data = policy_comparison_central)
```

```{r}
summary(policy_lm)
```

```{r}
summary(policy_gam)
```

```{r}
## for lm
# 1. Extract and Clean Coefficients
crop_coeffs <- tidy(policy_lm) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "crop_type_combine") & !str_detect(term, "Undefined")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate 95% Confidence Intervals
    lower = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    upper = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
    
    # Clean up term names: remove the prefix "crop_type_combine"
    term_clean = str_remove(term, "crop_type_combine")
  )

# 2. Create the Forest Plot
crop_plot <- ggplot(crop_coeffs, aes(x = estimate, y = term_clean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.2, color = "darkgreen") +
  
  # Add a vertical reference line at 0 (the value for "Forest")
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 0.8) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Effect of Crop Type on Policy (Central Area)",
    subtitle = "Relative to the 'Forest' base level",
    x = "Estimated Well Depth Policy Change (Log(Feet))",
    y = "Crop Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(crop_plot)
```


```{r}
### For gam
# 1. Extract and Clean Coefficients
crop_coeffs <- tidy(policy_gam, parametric = TRUE) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "crop_type_combine") & !str_detect(term, "Undefined")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate 95% Confidence Intervals
    lower = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    upper = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
    
    # Clean up term names: remove the prefix "crop_type_combine"
    term_clean = str_remove(term, "crop_type_combine")
  )

# 2. Create the Forest Plot
crop_plot <- ggplot(crop_coeffs, aes(x = estimate, y = term_clean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.2, color = "darkgreen") +
  
  # Add a vertical reference line at 0 (the value for "Forest")
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 0.8) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Effect of Crop Type on Policy (Central Area)",
    subtitle = "Relative to the 'Forest' base level",
    x = "Estimated Well Depth Policy Change (Log(Feet))",
    y = "Crop Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray80", size = 0.5, linetype = "dotted"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.25, linetype = "dotted"),
    
    # You are also setting the plot border/axis lines here:
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.5),
    # You might want to remove the border box drawn by theme_bw
    panel.border = element_blank()
  )

print(crop_plot)
```

```{r}
## For lm 
library(emmeans)

# 1. Calculate Estimated Marginal Means (Predicted Policy)
# This function automatically:
# - Holds continuous variables (precipitation, StaticLevel, U, cafolog) at their mean.
# - Holds the other categorical variable (crop_type_combine) at its reference level ("Forest").
emm_drainage <- emmeans(policy_lm, ~ drainagecl)

# 2. Convert to data frame for ggplot2
emm_df <- as.data.frame(emm_drainage) %>%
  # Ensure the drainage class is recognized as ordered for the plot
  mutate(drainagecl = factor(drainagecl, 
                             levels = levels(policy_comparison_central$drainagecl), 
                             ordered = TRUE))

# 3. Plot the ordered trend
drainage_plot <- ggplot(emm_df, aes(x = drainagecl, y = emmean)) +
  # Connect the means with a line to emphasize the trend (group=1 prevents unwanted segmentation)
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "darkblue", size = 1) + 
  # Add points for the estimated means
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkblue") +
  # Add 95% confidence intervals
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL), width = 0.2, color = "darkblue") +
  
  labs(
    title = "Well Depth Policy Across Soil Drainage Classes",
    # subtitle = "Non-linear trend shown across the ordered levels",
    x = "Soil Drainage Class (Ordered: Very Poorly → Excessively)",
    y = "Predicted Policy (Estimated Marginal Mean)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Rotate x-axis labels for readability
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(drainage_plot)
```

```{r}
## For Gam

# --- Ensure policy_gam is the GAM model object ---

# 1. Calculate Estimated Marginal Means (Predicted Policy) from the GAM
# The emmeans function handles the polynomial contrasts correctly for GAMs.
emm_drainage <- emmeans(policy_gam, ~ drainagecl)

# 2. Convert to data frame for ggplot2
emm_df <- as.data.frame(emm_drainage) %>%
  # Ensure the drainage class is recognized as ordered for the plot
  mutate(drainagecl = factor(drainagecl, 
                             levels = levels(policy_comparison_central$drainagecl), 
                             ordered = TRUE))

# 3. Plot the ordered trend
drainage_plot <- ggplot(emm_df, aes(x = drainagecl, y = emmean)) +
  # Connect the means with a line to emphasize the trend 
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "darkblue", size = 1) + 
  # Add points for the estimated means
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkblue") +
  # Add 95% confidence intervals
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL), width = 0.2, color = "darkblue") +
  
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Policy Value Across Soil Drainage Classes (GAM)",
    subtitle = "Trend adjusted for smooth effects of continuous covariates",
    x = "Soil Drainage Class (Ordered: Very Poorly → Excessively)",
    y = "Predicted Policy (Estimated Marginal Mean)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Rotate x-axis labels for readability
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(drainage_plot)
```


```{r}
draw(policy_gam, select = "s(U)", rug = FALSE, ci_alpha = 0.2) + 
  geom_line(color = "#0479A8", linewidth = 1.0) +
  # coord_cartesian(xlim = c(15000, 66000)) +  # Set the visible range for the x-axis
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Kriging Value (Gaussian process)", # Set custom x-axis label
    y = "Depth Policy (Log Feet)"         # Set custom y-axis label
  )+
  # theme_minimal() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        # panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
        )
```


```{r}
plot(ggpredict(policy_gam, terms = c("precipitation"))) + 
  labs(
    title = "Linear Effect of Precipitation",
    x = "precipitation (mm/day)",
    y = "Marginal Average of Policy (Log Feet)"
  ) +
  # scale_y_continuous(labels = exp)+
  theme_bw() # Change the whole theme

plot(ggpredict(policy_gam, terms = c("StaticLevel")))+ 
  labs(
    title = "Linear Effect of Static Level",
    x = "static level (feet)",
    y = "Marginal Average of Policy (Log Feet)",
  ) +
  theme_bw() # Change the whole theme
plot(ggpredict(policy_gam, terms = c("cafolog")))+ 
  labs(
    title = "Linear Effect of CAFO",
    x = "cafolog (mm/day)",
    y = "cafo",
  ) +
  theme_bw() # Change the whole theme
```


```{r}
# 1. Generate the predicted effects for your 3 terms
# This function calculates the predicted values for each term
all_effects <- ggpredict(policy_gam, terms = c("precipitation", "StaticLevel", "cafolog"))

# 2. Plot the effects
# This will automatically create a faceted plot with all 3 variables
plot(all_effects)
```

###### Visualize the projection of difference between direct and indirect policy on covariates

```{r}
policy_comparison_central$policy_diff <- policy_comparison_central$policy - policy_comparison_central$indirect_policy 
```

```{r}
# policy_diff_lm <- lm(policy_diff ~ crop_type_combine + precipitation + StaticLevel + drainagecl + U + cafolog, data = policy_comparison_central)

policy_diff_gam <- mgcv::gam(policy_diff ~ crop_type_combine + precipitation + StaticLevel + drainagecl + s(U) + cafolog, data = policy_comparison_central)
# policy_diff_lm <- lm(policy_diff ~ U, data = policy_comparison_central)
```

```{r}
summary(policy_diff_gam)
```


```{r}
summary(policy_gam)
```

```{r}
## for lm
# 1. Extract and Clean Coefficients
crop_coeffs <- tidy(policy_lm) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "crop_type_combine") & !str_detect(term, "Undefined")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate 95% Confidence Intervals
    lower = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    upper = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
    
    # Clean up term names: remove the prefix "crop_type_combine"
    term_clean = str_remove(term, "crop_type_combine")
  )

# 2. Create the Forest Plot
crop_plot <- ggplot(crop_coeffs, aes(x = estimate, y = term_clean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.2, color = "darkgreen") +
  
  # Add a vertical reference line at 0 (the value for "Forest")
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 0.8) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Effect of Crop Type on Policy (Central Area)",
    subtitle = "Relative to the 'Forest' base level",
    x = "Estimated Well Depth Policy Change (Log(Feet))",
    y = "Crop Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(crop_plot)
```


```{r}
### For gam
# 1. Extract and Clean Coefficients
crop_coeffs <- tidy(policy_gam, parametric = TRUE) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "crop_type_combine") & !str_detect(term, "Undefined")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate 95% Confidence Intervals
    lower = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    upper = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
    
    # Clean up term names: remove the prefix "crop_type_combine"
    term_clean = str_remove(term, "crop_type_combine")
  )

# 2. Create the Forest Plot
crop_plot <- ggplot(crop_coeffs, aes(x = estimate, y = term_clean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.2, color = "darkgreen") +
  
  # Add a vertical reference line at 0 (the value for "Forest")
```
