```{r}
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 0.8) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Effect of Crop Type on Policy (Central Area)",
    subtitle = "Relative to the 'Forest' base level",
    x = "Estimated Well Depth Policy Change (Log(Feet))",
    y = "Crop Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray80", size = 0.5, linetype = "dotted"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.25, linetype = "dotted"),
    
    # You are also setting the plot border/axis lines here:
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.5),
    # You might want to remove the border box drawn by theme_bw
    panel.border = element_blank()
  )

print(crop_plot)
```

###### well depth at different regions

```{r}
log_limits <- c(log(10), log(2000))

# Example Breaks (you can customize these)
# Let's use some nice round numbers for the *original* depth: 100, 500, 1000, 2000
original_depth_breaks <- c(10, 100, 500, 1000, 2000)
log_breaks <- log(original_depth_breaks)

# 2. Define the common scale object (optional, but cleaner)
common_scale <- scale_color_viridis_c(
  option = "plasma",
  name = "Well Depth (Feet)", # Use the name from the first plot for consistency
  limits = log_limits,
  breaks = log_breaks,
  labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) }
)
```

```{r}
ggplot()+
  # geom_sf(data = data_central_sf[data_central_sf$WellDepth!=5027,], aes(color = log(WellDepth)), size = 1.7) +
  geom_sf(data = data_central_sf, aes(color = log(WellDepth)), size = 1.7) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +
  # scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
  #                       name = "Well Depth (Feet)",
  #                       labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) }) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D",
                        name = "Well Depth (Feet)", # Use the name from the first plot for consistency
                        limits = log_limits,
                        breaks = log_breaks,
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
ggplot()+
  # geom_sf(data = data_central_sf[data_central_sf$WellDepth!=5027,], aes(color = log(WellDepth)), size = 1.7) +
  geom_sf(data = data_west_central_sf, aes(color = log(WellDepth)), size = 0.8) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_west_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +
  # scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
  #                       name = "Well Depth (Feet)",
  #                       labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) }) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D",
                        name = "Well Depth (Feet)", # Use the name from the first plot for consistency
                        limits = c(log(10), log(1000)),
                        breaks = log(c(10, 100, 500, 1000)),
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```


###### policy map at different regions

I think we need to winsorize the map, how should we winsorize the map


```{r}
plss_covariates_central_sf$policy_clip <- pmax(pmin(log(2000), plss_covariates_central_sf$policy), log(10))
```


```{r}
ggplot()+
  # geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_south_west_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  # geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  # geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_east_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  # geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_south_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf, aes(color = policy_clip), size = 1.7) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +
  # scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
  #                       name = "Minimum Depth (Feet)",
  #                       labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D",
                        name = "Well Depth (Feet)", # Use the name from the first plot for consistency
                        limits = log_limits,
                        breaks = log_breaks,
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color = policy), size = 0.8) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_west_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +
  # scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
  #                       name = "Minimum Depth (Feet)",
  #                       labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) })+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D",
                        name = "Well Depth (Feet)", # Use the name from the first plot for consistency
                        limits = c(log(10), log(1000)),
                        breaks = log(c(10, 100, 500, 1000)),
                        labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "5mg/L Threshold Well Depth", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```


###### nitrate map at different regions

```{r}
ggplot()+
  # geom_sf(data = data_central_sf[data_central_sf$WellDepth!=5027,], aes(color = log(WellDepth)), size = 1.7) +
  geom_sf(data = data_central_sf, aes(color = log(concentration_plus_median+0.5)), size = 1.7) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +
  # scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
  #                       name = "Well Depth (Feet)",
  #                       labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) }) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Nitrate (mg/L)", # Use the name from the first plot for consistency
                        limits = c(log(1),log(55)),
                        breaks = log(c(0,5,10,20,35,50)),
                        labels = function(breaks) {round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "Nitrate Concentration", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
ggplot()+
  # geom_sf(data = data_central_sf[data_central_sf$WellDepth!=5027,], aes(color = log(WellDepth)), size = 1.7) +
  geom_sf(data = data_west_central_sf, aes(color = log(concentration_plus_median+0.5)), size = 0.8) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_west_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +
  # scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
  #                       name = "Well Depth (Feet)",
  #                       labels = function(breaks) { round(exp(breaks), 0) }) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        name = "Nitrate (mg/L)", # Use the name from the first plot for consistency
                        limits = c(log(1),log(55)),
                        breaks = log(c(0,5,10,20,35,55)),
                        labels = function(breaks) {round(exp(breaks), 0)}) +
  labs(title = "Nitrate Concentration", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_void()
```

###### spatial covariate at different regions

####### CAFO Plot

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +  
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf, aes(color= log(cafo+1)),size=1) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma",name = "Ln(CAFO+1)") +  # Color scale
  labs(title = "CAFO", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = counties_west_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +  
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color= log(cafo+1)),size=1) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma",name = "Ln(CAFO+1)") +  # Color scale
  labs(title = "CAFO") +
  theme_void()
```


####### Land Use


```{r}
# land use/crop type
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +  
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf[!(plss_covariates_central_sf$crop_type_combine %in% c("Undefined","Vegtables","Cover Crop","Other Crop")),], aes(color=crop_type_combine ),size=1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Forest" = "darkgreen", 
      "Grass" = "lightgreen", 
      "Corn" = "red",  # Make "Corn" stand out with red
      "Developed" = "gray", 
      "Soybeans" = "blue",  # Make "Soybeans" stand out with blue
      "Other Crop" = "orange",  # Make "Other Crop" stand out with orange
      "Cover Crop" = "brown", 
      "Vegtables" = "purple"
    ),
    name = "Land Use"
  ) + 
  # scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma", name = "Crop Type") +  # Color scale
  # labs(title = "Log(Cafo)", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Land Use", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      keywidth = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase width of the legend squares
      keyheight = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase height of the legend squares
      override.aes = list(shape = 15, size = 7)  # Set legend keys to squares (shape 15) and increase size
    )
  )
```

```{r}
# land use/crop type
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = counties_west_central,color = 'black', size = 1) +  
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf[!(plss_covariates_west_central_sf$crop_type_combine %in% c("Undefined","Vegtables","Cover Crop","Other Crop")),], aes(color=crop_type_combine ),size=0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Forest" = "darkgreen", 
      "Grass" = "lightgreen", 
      "Corn" = "red",  # Make "Corn" stand out with red
      "Developed" = "gray", 
      "Soybeans" = "blue",  # Make "Soybeans" stand out with blue
      "Other Crop" = "orange",  # Make "Other Crop" stand out with orange
      "Cover Crop" = "brown", 
      "Vegtables" = "purple"
    ),
    name = "Land Use"
  ) + 
  # scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma", name = "Crop Type") +  # Color scale
  # labs(title = "Log(Cafo)", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Land Use", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      keywidth = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase width of the legend squares
      keyheight = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase height of the legend squares
      override.aes = list(shape = 15, size = 7)  # Set legend keys to squares (shape 15) and increase size
    )
  )
```

####### Precipitation Plot

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = 'black', size = 1,fill = 'white') +  
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf, aes(color= precipitation),size=1) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = 'white',  # Light blue
  high = "skyblue", # Dark blue
  name = "mm/day")+
  labs(title = "Precipitation", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = counties_west_central,color = 'black', size = 1,fill = 'white') +  
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color= precipitation),size=1) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = 'white',  # Light blue
  high = "skyblue", # Dark blue
  name = "mm/day")+
  labs(title = "Precipitation", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal()
```

####### Soil Drainage Plot

```{r}
data_factor <- factor(data,
  levels = c("Excessively drained", "Well drained", "Moderately well drained", "Somewhat poorly drained", "Poorly drained", "Very poorly drained")
)
```

```{r}
# plss_covariates_sf_drainage = plss_covariates_sf[!is.na(plss_covariates_sf$drainagecl),]
plss_covariates_central_sf$drainagecl = factor(plss_covariates_central_sf$drainagecl,
  levels = c("Very poorly drained","Poorly drained","Somewhat poorly drained","Moderately well drained","Well drained","Somewhat excessively drained","Excessively drained"))
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = counties_central,color = 'black', size = 1,fill = 'lightgrey') +  
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_central_sf, aes(color=drainagecl),size=1) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "magma", name = "Soil Drainage Level",
                     labels = c(
                          "Very poorly drained" = "Very poor",  # Custom label
                          "Poorly drained" = "Poorly",                  # Custom label
                          "Somewhat poorly drained" = "Somewhat poor",              # Custom label
                          "Moderately well drained" = "Moderately well",         # Custom label
                          "Well drained" = "Well",
                          "Somewhat excessively drained" = "Somewhat excessive",
                          "Excessively drained" = "Excessive"
    )) + 
  theme_minimal()+
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      keywidth = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase width of the legend squares
      keyheight = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase height of the legend squares
      override.aes = list(shape = 15, size = 7)  # Set legend keys to squares (shape 15) and increase size
    )
  )
```


```{r}
# plss_covariates_sf_drainage = plss_covariates_sf[!is.na(plss_covariates_sf$drainagecl),]
plss_covariates_west_central_sf$drainagecl = factor(plss_covariates_west_central_sf$drainagecl,
  levels = c("Very poorly drained","Poorly drained","Somewhat poorly drained","Moderately well drained","Well drained","Somewhat excessively drained","Excessively drained"))
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = plss_covariates_west_central_sf, aes(color=drainagecl),size=0.7) +
  geom_sf(data = counties_west_central,color = 'black', size = 1,fill = 'lightgrey') +  
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "magma", name = "Soil Drainage Level",
                     labels = c(
                          "Very poorly drained" = "Very poor",  # Custom label
                          "Poorly drained" = "Poorly",                  # Custom label
                          "Somewhat poorly drained" = "Somewhat poor",              # Custom label
                          "Moderately well drained" = "Moderately well",         # Custom label
                          "Well drained" = "Well",
                          "Somewhat excessively drained" = "Somewhat excessive",
                          "Excessively drained" = "Excessive"
    )) + 
  theme_minimal()+
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      keywidth = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase width of the legend squares
      keyheight = unit(0.7, "cm"),  # Increase height of the legend squares
      override.aes = list(shape = 15, size = 7)  # Set legend keys to squares (shape 15) and increase size
    )
  )
```
